content.text:
{Whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men.}{Colossians 3:23}
 {It is by logic that we prove, but by intuition that we discover.}{— Henri Poincaré}
Eigenvalues appear throughout science and engineering.

Consider $\vect{x}_{k+1} = A\vect{x}_k$. If $A = PDP\inv$:
\[
\vect{x}_k = A^k\vect{x}_0 = PD^kP\inv\vect{x}_0
\]
The dominant eigenvalue determines long-term behavior.

content.title: Fibonacci sequence
content.problem:
The Fibonacci recursion $F_{n+1} = F_n + F_{n-1}$ can be written as:
\[
\begin{bmatrix} F_{n+1} \\ F_n \end{bmatrix} = 
\begin{bmatrix} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{bmatrix}
\begin{bmatrix} F_n \\ F_{n-1} \end{bmatrix}
\]
The eigenvalues are $\phi = \frac{1+\sqrt{5}}{2}$ (golden ratio) and 
$\psi = \frac{1-\sqrt{5}}{2}$. This gives the closed form:
\[
F_n = \frac{\phi^n - \psi^n}{\sqrt{5}}
\]

content.text:
For $\vect{x}' = A\vect{x}$, solutions involve $e^{\lambda t}$ where 
$\lambda$ are eigenvalues of $A$.

In data science, eigenvalues of covariance matrices identify principal 
directions of variation.

appointed time; it hastens to the end—it will not lie.
